description_long: This object consists of four upright stone blocks of varying heights and widths, arranged side by side on a rectangular stone base. The stones have a weathered, rough surface with visible chipping and irregularities, suggesting significant age and exposure to the elements. The front faces of the three larger stones display shallow relief carvings, possibly depicting human figures or deities, while the smallest stone is more angular and less detailed. The coloration is predominantly a pale gray with subtle hints of mossy green and brown, indicating some biological growth and staining. The overall form is monolithic and solid, with each stone appearing to be carved from a single piece of rock. This arrangement is typical of traditional guard stones found at the entrances to historical sites or along old urban pathways.
description: A group of four weathered stone guard stones with relief carvings, set on a rectangular base and showing signs of age and exposure.
description_view_0: The front view shows all four stones, highlighting their varying heights and the rough, unadorned surfaces.
description_view_1: The side view reveals the thinness of the stones and their alignment in a straight row.
description_view_2: The rear view displays the relief carvings on the three larger stones and the plain surface of the smallest stone.
description_view_3: The opposite side view again shows the narrow profile and the depth of the carvings on the stones.
attribute_location: Typically placed at the entrance to historical sites, along old urban streets, or at the corners of sidewalks to protect structures from vehicle impact.
attribute_installation_orientation: Meant to be installed upright, partially embedded in the ground for stability, with the carved faces oriented toward the street or pathway.
attribute_wear_patterns: Visible chipping and erosion on the edges and corners, with surface smoothing and discoloration likely caused by weathering, vehicle contact, and occasional foot traffic. Moss and lichen growth is present in crevices and lower areas.
attribute_historical_markings: Shallow relief carvings depicting human figures or deities on the front faces of the three larger stones; no visible inscriptions or text.
